message:
fix(pt-redact): Correct path and URL canonicalization edge cases

Fix two bugs in the Canonicalizer that caused inconsistent output:

1. Temp directory pattern ordering (canonicalize_path):
   - BEFORE: Generic /tmp/ replacement ran first, then session pattern
     regex replaced within the already-transformed path, causing
     double-transformation like "[TMP]/pytest-123" → "[TMP]/[TMP]"
   - AFTER: Session-specific patterns (pytest-*, session-*, etc.) are
     matched first against the original path, producing "[TMP]/rest".
     Only if no session pattern matches does the generic /tmp/ fallback
     apply.
   - This ensures deterministic canonicalization regardless of whether
     a path contains session identifiers.

2. URL placeholder case preservation (canonicalize_url):
   - BEFORE: Entire URL was lowercased including placeholders, turning
     "https://[CRED]@host:[PORT]/path" into "https://[cred]@host:[port]/path"
   - AFTER: Lowercase transformation skips content within square brackets,
     preserving "[CRED]" and "[PORT]" as canonical uppercase tokens while
     still normalizing the rest of the URL.
   - This maintains consistency with other redaction placeholders like
     [REDACTED], [PII], etc. which use uppercase by convention.

Both fixes improve reproducibility of redacted output for:
- Session artifact comparisons (config snapshots, telemetry)
- Test fixture stability (golden file tests)
- Log aggregation and deduplication

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
